metric_name: Microsoft has 'silent friends' in global industry
metric_value: it will be interesting to note which companies emerge as allies to the Redmond-based giant; Microsoft has many 'silent friends' who might become vocal as the case goes forward
notes: Kastner direct quote to E-Commerce Times

metric_name: Different EU business standard threatens every global company
metric_value: How Microsoft does business has been determined as legal in the U.S. So, for the EU to set a totally different standard for business conduct threatens not only Microsoft, but every other global company as well.

metric_name: Microsoft chose appeal over payment to clarify law
metric_value: They could have paid. Doing an appeal was not their only option. But paying would have meant they couldn't address the issues of law and commerce that they're seeking to clarify now.
5 years of EU investigation preceded the ruling
$2B Microsoft payment to Sun for technology licensing in Apr 2004
notes: Pre-appeal settlement raised questions about appeal strength

metric_name: Microsoft fine will be paid at last possible minute
metric_value: It's quite a chunk of change. They won't pay it one minute before it's due.
about as much chance of success as it did before. It's a long shot. But they'll definitely fight every step of the way.

metric_name: CFI upheld EC ruling against Microsoft
metric_value: Court of First Instance ruled against Microsoft on 2007-09-17, upholding EC's antitrust decision and fine
notes: Validates DiDio 'long shot' prediction; Microsoft ultimately lost; dropped final appeal 2012

metric_name: EU continues expanding Big Tech antitrust precedent
metric_value: Digital Markets Act (DMA) 2022 entered force Mar 2024; designated Microsoft/Apple/Google/Meta/Amazon/ByteDance as gatekeepers; fines against Google cumulative $8.25B through 2023; Apple €1.8B DMA fine 2024
notes: Validates Kastner 'threatens every global company' framing

metric_name: EC remedies shape industry behavior long-term
metric_value: the impact of the commission's decision goes beyond Microsoft and toward other companies
notes: Desler (MS spokesperson) quote; validated over 20 years
